# Aevum

> The governed context kernel for AI agents.
> Replay-first. Policy-enforced. Cryptographically auditable.
> Apache-2.0 — https://github.com/aevum-labs/aevum

## What Aevum is

Aevum is an open-source AI context kernel that sits between AI agents and
the data they access. It enforces policy on every memory read and write,
records every decision in a tamper-evident sigchain, and makes any past
agent session deterministically replayable.

It is not a memory store. It is not an observability platform.
It is the governance and auditability layer underneath both.

## Five public functions

- ingest: policy-checked data ingestion with provenance and classification
- query: consent-gated, classification-ceiling-enforced context retrieval
- review: human-in-the-loop gate before irreversible agent actions
- commit: episodic event recording in the immutable sigchain ledger
- replay: deterministic reconstruction of any past agent session

## Five absolute barriers (cannot be bypassed by any complication)

1. Crisis detection — crisis content stops immediately, always
2. Classification ceiling — agents cannot access data above their clearance
3. Consent enforcement — no data access without a valid, scoped consent grant
4. Audit immutability — the sigchain cannot be modified or deleted
5. Provenance chain — every fact carries its full chain of custody

## Compliance relevance

- EU AI Act Article 12: sigchain provides automatic, tamper-evident recording
- ISO/IEC 42001: complication lifecycle and governance controls
- SOC 2 PI1.2: provenance and classification ceiling enforcement
- OWASP ASI06 (Memory Poisoning): policy-mediated writes prevent injection

## Agent autonomy levels

Aevum enforces L1 (operator-controlled) through L5 (observer-only) autonomy
levels as defined in the DeepMind taxonomy. Level is enforced by policy,
not by the agent itself.

## Install

pip install aevum-core

## Documentation

Full docs:       https://aevum.build
Getting started: https://aevum.build/getting-started/quickstart/
How it works:    https://aevum.build/concepts/how-it-works/
API reference:   https://aevum.build/reference/engine/
